zh.Oostvoorne, South Holland, Netherlands: 荷蘭-拉藍德奧斯特賀內
zh.Oostvoorne, Zuid-Holland, Nederland: 荷蘭-拉藍德奧斯特賀內
zh.lifeSummary: 當Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland於1280年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德奧斯特賀內時，他的父親Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland44歲，母親Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy25歲。他於大約1300年與Hadewich van Borselen結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1337年4月20日在他的家鄉享年57歲。

zh-hans.Oostvoorne, South Holland, Netherlands: 荷兰-拉藍德奧斯特賀內
zh-hans.Oostvoorne, Zuid-Holland, Nederland: 荷兰-拉藍德奧斯特賀內
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland于1280年出生在荷兰-拉藍德奧斯特賀內时，他的父亲Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland44岁，母亲Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy25岁。他于大约1300年与Hadewich van Borselen结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1337年4月20日在他的家乡享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’. In the later Middle Ages this was a more common name than Gerald , with which it was sometimes confused. Nowadays it is less common, surviving mainly among Roman Catholics, in honour of the many saints of this name.

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: Variant spelling of   Gerard  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Jerrard
meanings: Rare variant spelling of   Gerard  , probably influenced by the form of the modern surname, if not a transferred use.

en.lifeSummary: When Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland was born in 1280, in Oostvoorne, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland, was 44 and his mother, Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy, was 25. He married Hadewich van Borselen about 1300. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 20 April 1337, in his hometown, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland est né en 1280, à Oostvoorne, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Hadewich van Borselen environ 1300. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 20 avril 1337, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland 1280 in Oostvoorne, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland 44 und seine Mutter Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy 25. Er hat um 1300 Hadewich van Borselen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 20. April 1337 im Alter von 57 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland è nato il nel 1280 nel Oostvoorne, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Hadewich van Borselen circa 1300. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 20 aprile 1337, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeelandが1280年にオランダズイド-hオランドオオストボオルンで生まれた時，父Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeelandは44歳，母Catharina van Limburg van Durbuyは25歳であった。約1300年にHadewich van Borselenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1337年4月20日に故郷で57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland(이)가 1280년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 스트보른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1300년 Hadewich van Borselen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1337년 4월 20일 그의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland nasceu em 1280, em Oostvoorne, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Hadewich van Borselen aproximadamente 1300. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 20 de abril de 1337, em sua cidade natal, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland родился в 1280 г. в местности уствурн, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Hadewich van Borselen около 1300 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 20 апреля 1337 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gerard heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland nació en 1280, en Oostvoorne, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Albrecht heer van Voorne burggraaf van Zeeland, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Catharina van Limburg van Durbuy, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Hadewich van Borselen aproximadamente 1300. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 20 de abril de 1337, en su pueblo, a la edad de 57 años.
